name: Chicken with Water Chestnuts
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Water chestnuts, fresh or canned (drained weight)
2 tsp Rice wine or dry sherry
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT THE CHICKEN into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. COMBINE it with the egg white, salt and cornstarch in a small bowl. Place the mixture in the refrigerator for about 20 minutes.
3. IF USING FRESH WATER CHESTNUTS, peel them. IF USING CANNED WATER CHESTNUTS, drain them well and rinse in cold water. Coarsely chop the water chestnuts.
4. HEAT a wok or large saute pan until hot. ADD the oil.
5. WHEN THE OIL IS MODERATELY HOT, ADD the chicken cubes and STIR to prevent sticking.
6. WHEN THE CHICKEN PIECES TURN WHITE (ABOUT 2 MINUTES), QUICKLY DRAIN THE CHICKEN AND ALL THE OIL INTO A STAINLESS STEEL COLANDER SET IN A BOWL.
7. CLEAN THE WOK AND REHEAT IT. RETURN 2 TABLESPOONS OF OIL TO THE WOK.
8. ADD THE GARLIC AND GINGER, and STIR-FRY FOR 30 SECONDS.
9. THEN ADD THE WATER CHESTNUTS, CHILI BEAN SAUCE, SOY SAUCE, RICE WINE, SUGAR, AND SALT. CONTINUE TO STIR-FRY FOR 1 MINUTE.
10. RETURN THE CHICKEN TO THE WOK and COOK FOR ANOTHER 2 MINUTES.
11. ADD THE SESAME OIL, GIVE THE MIXTURE A FINAL STIR, and SERVE AT ONCE.
